text: Mobile app development involves creating software for smartphones and tablets. One key consideration is responsive design, which ensures the app works well on different screen sizes, from small phones to large tablets. Developers also choose between native and cross-platform approaches. Native apps are built for a specific system, like iOS or Android, offering better performance and access to device features. Cross-platform tools, like React Native, allow a single codebase to run on both systems, saving time but sometimes sacrificing smoother performance. After development, apps are distributed through app stores, such as the Apple App Store or Google Play, where they must meet strict guidelines to reach users.
